factors.summary: Overall demand appears to be decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and economic slowdown.

evidence.text: We are carefully monitoring the COVID-19 pandemic and the resulting length and depth of the economic impacts on our financial condition and results of operations. In early March 2020, we cancelled all of our large customer events scheduled for the second quarter of 2020. We estimated the cancellation of those events will reduce non-recurring revenue by approximately $50 million. We are also experiencing pressure from an economic slowdown, from significantly lower oil prices and related decrease in capital expenditure spending, and a near-term shock to consumer spending impacting our automotive customers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing decelerating demand due to the COVID-19 pandemic and economic slowdown.

factors.summary: The company is successfully holding prices, with evidence of organic revenue growth across segments.

evidence.text: We saw solid organic revenue growth in our Transportation and Financial Services segments for the three months ended February 29, 2020 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2019 .

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully holding prices, indicated by organic revenue growth across segments.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting with recurring fixed revenue growing at a higher rate than non-recurring revenue, which is a positive trend.

evidence.text: Recurring fixed revenue organic growth increased 7 percent for the three months ended February 29, 2020 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2019 , largely due to contributions from our Transportation and Financial Services recurring offerings, with slight organic growth in Resources and CMS.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting favorably with recurring fixed revenue growing faster than non-recurring revenue.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on certain segments.

evidence.text: As a result of the anticipated adverse revenue impacts, we are implementing cost reduction efforts to partially offset the revenue decline. Such cost reduction efforts will include both fixed and variable costs across all areas of our business.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing, particularly in the Transportation and Financial Services segments, but face near-term challenges due to the pandemic.

evidence.text: We saw solid organic revenue growth in our Transportation and Financial Services segments for the three months ended February 29, 2020 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2019 .

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes or transaction counts are growing in key segments, though facing near-term pandemic challenges.

factors.summary: The Transportation and Financial Services segments are growing fastest, while Resources and CMS face slower growth due to external factors.

evidence.text: Financial Services revenue for the three months ended February 29, 2020 , compared to the three months ended February 28, 2019 , increased organically from strength across our Information, Solutions, and Processing product offerings. Within our Information product offerings, organic revenue growth was led by our core pricing, valuation, equities, and indices offerings, as well as our advisory solutions. Within our Solutions product offerings, organic revenue growth was led by our WSO, issuer services (particularly serving equity and municipal issuances), and private capital markets offerings. Within our Processing product offerings, organic revenue growth was led by derivative processing activity.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Transportation and Financial Services segments are growing fastest, indicating strong end-market momentum.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and economic slowdown.

evidence.text: We are carefully monitoring the COVID-19 pandemic and the resulting length and depth of the economic impacts on our financial condition and results of operations. In early March 2020, we cancelled all of our large customer events scheduled for the second quarter of 2020. We estimated the cancellation of those events will reduce non-recurring revenue by approximately $50 million. We are also experiencing pressure from an economic slowdown, from significantly lower oil prices and related decrease in capital expenditure spending, and a near-term shock to consumer spending impacting our automotive customers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management is cautious due to the COVID-19 pandemic and economic slowdown, indicating potential challenges ahead.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include reduced non-recurring revenue due to event cancellations, economic slowdown impacts on automotive and resources segments, and lower oil prices affecting customers.

evidence.text: We estimated the cancellation of those events will reduce non-recurring revenue by approximately $50 million. We are also experiencing pressure from an economic slowdown, from significantly lower oil prices and related decrease in capital expenditure spending, and a near-term shock to consumer spending impacting our automotive customers. A number of our small- and mid-size oil and gas customers will be negatively impacted by this development, which may result in a decrease in the amount of products and services they may purchase from us, particularly in our Resources Upstream product offerings. The economic slowdown and decrease in consumer spending pressures are also causing a near-term disruption in the car-buying process, which is impacting our North American automotive dealer customers in a manner that is not representative of a typical cyclical downturn.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include revenue reduction and economic impacts on key segments, suggesting challenges in the short term.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on sustainable, profitable growth across its industry segments, supported by investments in innovation and strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: We believe that this sales and operating model helps our customers do business with us by providing a cohesive, consistent, and effective product, sales, and marketing approach by segment. For the first quarter of 2020, we focused our efforts on the following actions: Increase in geographic, product, and customer penetration. We believe there are continued opportunities to add new customers and to increase the use of our products and services by existing customers. We plan to add new customers and build our relationships with existing customers by leveraging our existing sales channels, broad product portfolio, global footprint, and industry expertise to anticipate and respond to the changing demands of our end markets. Introduce innovative offerings and enhancements. In recent years, we have launched several new product offerings addressing a wide array of customer needs, and we expect to continue to innovate using our existing data sets and industry expertise, converting core information to higher value advanced analytics. Our investment priorities are primarily in energy, automotive, and financial services, and we intend to continue to invest across our business to increase our customer value proposition. Balance capital allocation. We will continue to manage to our capital policy target leverage ratio, and intend to return 50 to 75 percent of our annual capital capacity to shareholders through share repurchases and a quarterly dividend. We will continue to evaluate the long-term potential and strategic fit of our asset portfolio, and we will also continue to evaluate potential mergers and acquisitions, focused primarily on targeted transactions in our core end markets that will allow us to continue to build out our strategic position.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear, focused long-term strategy with investments in innovation and strategic acquisitions, indicating future growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include foreign exchange movements, economic conditions, and oil prices impacting the Resources and Transportation segments.

evidence.text: Approximately 40 percent of our revenue is transacted outside of the United States; however, only about 20 percent of our revenue is transacted in currencies other than the U.S. dollar. As a result, a strengthening U.S. dollar relative to certain currencies has historically resulted in a negative impact on our revenue; conversely, a weakening U.S. dollar has historically resulted in a positive impact on our revenue. Our largest foreign currency exposures for revenue are the British Pound, Euro, and Canadian Dollar. The economic slowdown and decrease in consumer spending pressures are also causing a near-term disruption in the car-buying process, which is impacting our North American automotive dealer customers in a manner that is not representative of a typical cyclical downturn. We are anticipating incremental adverse revenue impacts from the current environment in our Resources segment (primarily related to our Upstream product offerings) and our Transportation segment (primarily as a result of an anticipated decline in consumer automotive spending and related impact to our automotive dealer customers).

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Sensitivities to foreign exchange, economic conditions, and oil prices pose risks to key segments, indicating vulnerabilities.

factors.summary: Potential catalysts include the introduction of innovative product offerings and the success of cost reduction efforts.

evidence.text: For the first quarter of 2020, we focused our efforts on the following actions: Introduce innovative offerings and enhancements. In recent years, we have launched several new product offerings addressing a wide array of customer needs, and we expect to continue to innovate using our existing data sets and industry expertise, converting core information to higher value advanced analytics. Our investment priorities are primarily in energy, automotive, and financial services, and we intend to continue to invest across our business to increase our customer value proposition. As a result of the anticipated adverse revenue impacts, we are implementing cost reduction efforts to partially offset the revenue decline. Such cost reduction efforts will include both fixed and variable costs across all areas of our business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Introduction of innovative products and cost reduction efforts can drive growth and improve profitability.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining strong market share, particularly in the Financial Services and Transportation segments, which are key to its recurring revenue.

evidence.text: We have more than 50,000 business and government customers, including 80 percent of the Fortune Global 500 and the world’s leading financial institutions. Headquartered in London, we are committed to sustainable, profitable growth. We believe that this sales and operating model helps our customers do business with us by providing a cohesive, consistent, and effective product, sales, and marketing approach by segment.

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The company's differentiation lies in its industry-focused segments, broad product portfolio, and long-term customer relationships, which provide a competitive advantage.

evidence.text: We are a world leader in critical information, analytics, and solutions for the major industries and markets that drive economies worldwide. We deliver next-generation information, analytics, and solutions to customers in business, finance, and government, improving their operational efficiency and providing deep insights that lead to well-informed, confident decisions. We have more than 50,000 business and government customers, including 80 percent of the Fortune Global 500 and the world’s leading financial institutions. Headquartered in London, we are committed to sustainable, profitable growth. We believe that this sales and operating model helps our customers do business with us by providing a cohesive, consistent, and effective product, sales, and marketing approach by segment.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company demonstrates strong differentiation through industry focus, product diversity, and customer relationships, providing a clear competitive advantage.

factors.summary: M&A activity includes the divestiture of the A&D business line and potential targeted acquisitions in core end markets.

evidence.text: We completed the divestiture of our Aerospace & Defense (“A&D”) business line for approximately $466 million, which was previously included in our Transportation segment. Proceeds from the divestiture were primarily used to fund our first quarter accelerated share repurchase (“ASR”). We will continue to evaluate potential mergers and acquisitions, focused primarily on targeted transactions in our core end markets that will allow us to continue to build out our strategic position.

sentiment.label: neutral
